title: Index Multiple Selection Delivery
summary: Shipped explicit manage-index checkbox selection as independent browser state without changing drag/drop or mutation contracts.
content_html:
Index Multiple Selection Delivery

Outcome

The manage-mode tree index has an explicit selection mode with row checkboxes, Shift-range selection, a selected count, Select all, Clear, and Done.

Checkbox selection is browser-only state independent from the displayed document. It does not change link navigation, the active-row highlight, current single-document controls, drag/drop, or backend mutation contracts.

This is the complete visible selection foundation. The later package-preparation delivery added the generic Select all transition without changing Action targeting, drag/drop, or lifecycle ownership. Group movement remains a separate decision.

Selection Contract

  • A registered manage-only Select control appears only for the index-tree view.
  • Entering selection mode reveals a checkbox gutter immediately left of every eligible document node.
  • The gutter and checkbox are siblings of the node. They do not move or narrow the node, wrap its title, replace its status slot, or begin drag/drop.
  • The selection owner holds selected document ids, the range anchor, and whether selection mode is active. Consumers never reconstruct selection by reading checkbox DOM state.
  • Clicking a checkbox changes only selection membership. Shift-click selects the inclusive visible range from the stable anchor in current tree order.
  • The checkbox is the complete checked-state indicator. Only the displayed document keeps the existing active-row highlight and aria-current="page".
  • Document links remain ordinary links. Navigation, native modifier-click, row focus, context-menu opening, and expansion changes do not alter selected ids.
  • The index control reports the count and exposes Select all, Clear, and Done. Select all replaces membership with every id in the canonical manage index, including collapsed and non-viewable documents, and clears the range anchor. It is disabled when that complete set is already checked.
  • Clear empties the set and remains in selection mode; Done exits and clears it.
  • Selection changes perform no request, write, rebuild, busy state, or cache invalidation.

Action-Target Isolation

Feed the checked ids into the existing code-owned action context, but do not let that silently retarget current single-document controls.

Prepare package is the one shipped selection consumer and declares the checked set as its only document target. Every other toolbar, Actions-menu, context-menu, and drag action continues to resolve its existing displayed, scope, or explicit invocation target. No destructive or mutation action becomes plural here.

The later group-movement delivery may make the move action consume the checked set. It must not recover a hidden primary selection or broaden another action as a shortcut.

Reload And Transition Contract

event selection result
Initial boot, browser reload, scope change, loss of management context, or leaving index-tree Start or return with selection mode off and an empty set. Do not persist selection in storage or across scopes.
Document-link navigation, history navigation within the same scope, focus, context menu, expansion, Search, or Recent Preserve selection mode, selected ids, and range anchor. These do not replace the index population.
Same-scope index reload after create/import, source save, metadata change, manual rebuild, settings change, viewability change, or committed-move recovery Preserve selected ids that remain eligible in the refreshed manage index; prune missing or newly ineligible ids; retain the anchor only while its document remains eligible.
Successful local single-document move Preserve the selection exactly. The existing committed local projection remains the normal path and does not reload the index.
Delete followed by index reload Remove the deleted or otherwise missing ids and preserve every remaining eligible id. Stay in selection mode even if the set becomes empty.
Clear or Done Clear empties membership and anchor; Done also exits selection mode.

A Shift-click whose previous anchor is no longer in the current visible range behaves as an ordinary checkbox toggle and establishes the new anchor. Reload reconciliation is one selection-owner operation, not case-specific state spread through every mutation workflow.

Delivery Tracker

Current state: complete. IMS-1 through IMS-4 shipped the visible checkbox-selection foundation, lifecycle reconciliation, action-target isolation, and focused acceptance evidence.

Next checkpoint: none. Document-package integration and Select all shipped through their own delivery; group movement still requires a separate approved delivery.

[x] IMS-1 — Establish Selection State And Target Isolation

  • [x] Add one focused manage-only selection owner with pure enter, toggle, range, clear, exit, and reload-reconcile transitions.
  • [x] Keep selected ids, range anchor, displayed document, focus, context row, and drag state separate.
  • [x] Pass the checked set into action resolution while preserving every current single-document action target.
  • [x] Add focused state and action-resolution evidence before projecting new controls.

Checkpoint evidence: docs-viewer-index-selection.js owns immutable state transitions inside the lazy manage-only controller. Current actions resolve active or invocation documents identically with zero, one, or several checked ids. The focused selection module smoke, existing manage-route smoke, static/public runtime tests, Python compilation, and repository-whitespace check pass.

[x] IMS-2 — Project The Selection Interaction

  • [x] Register the manage-only Select control through the existing index-view control host.
  • [x] Project controlled row checkboxes through a narrow sidebar seam without transferring selection ownership to the shared renderer.
  • [x] Add the count, Clear, and Done states and route checkbox events through the management interaction owner.
  • [x] Preserve document links, native modifier-click, active-row semantics, node drag behavior, and stable node layout.

Checkpoint evidence: docs_viewer_index_selection_modules.py proves control registration, sibling-gutter placement, stable link geometry, checkbox projection, inclusive visible ranges, independent active-document state, and retained drag handles. The router, tree-move, drag/drop, static-asset, and live manage-route checks pass. In-app browser review confirmed the compact gutter, three-row Shift range, count, Clear, and Done with an unchanged URL and active row and no console errors.

[x] IMS-3 — Reconcile Reloads And Lifecycle Changes

  • [x] Route every same-scope index replacement through one reload reconciliation boundary.
  • [x] Preserve or prune ids and the range anchor according to the table above.
  • [x] Clear selection when its scope, management context, or owning index view is left.
  • [x] Keep the normal committed single-move projection reload-free and selection-preserving.

Checkpoint evidence: the shared route workflow emits one index-replacement handoff after every successful index population change; the manage-only selection owner performs context sync and reload reconciliation. Focused owner evidence covers scope and management-context exit. The real manage-route lifecycle smoke covers Search and Recent preservation, same-scope reload pruning and missing-anchor behavior, index-view exit, and full browser reload with no persistence. The committed-move route smoke preserves the exact checked set and confirms the normal move performs no index reload; the existing recovery path converges on the same replacement boundary. Public runtime and static-asset boundary tests pass.

[x] IMS-4 — Verify And Record The Boundary

  • [x] Run focused pure-state, action-target, management-module, DOM/browser, public-boundary, syntax, and repository-whitespace checks.
  • [x] Confirm public, review, Search, Recent, graph, and mobile surfaces acquire no selection contract.
  • [x] Perform user visual and keyboard review for gutter fit, focus visibility, checkbox labels, count clarity, and Shift-range feel.
  • [x] Update the durable runtime owner with the shipped selection boundary and close this delivery without marking group movement complete.

Checkpoint evidence: the focused selection, lifecycle, tree-move, index-panel, router, and manage-route browser checks pass; public runtime and static-asset boundaries pass 15 tests; changed Python and JavaScript syntax checks and git diff --check pass. Public and review composition do not import the manage owner; Search and Recent preserve state without gaining selection controls; graph exit clears the owner; no mobile-specific, responsive, touch, or persistence contract was added. Live route review confirmed document-specific checkbox labels, polite count output, visible 2px keyboard focus, clean Done exit, and no console errors. User review confirmed the gutter and Shift-range feel; the existing hit area and Safari's native Shift-link Reading List feedback are deliberately unchanged. Runtime Module Ownership now records docs-viewer-index-selection.js as the focused owner. Group movement remains unimplemented and unapproved.

Not In Scope

  • group drag/drop, plural move payloads, effective move roots, destination validation, writes, rebuilds, or local multi-move projection;
  • package preparation or another multi-document consumer inside the original IMS delivery; Package Prepare shipped later through its own approved deliveries;
  • batch delete, viewability, metadata, copy, export, or a general batch-action framework;
  • modifier-click selection, keyboard range extension, direct toggle shortcuts, lasso, touch drag/drop, sibling reordering, or cross-scope selection;
  • selection persistence in local storage, session storage, URLs, packages, or backend state;
  • public, review, Search, Recent, graph, or mobile selection UI;
  • a broader index, toolbar, or Docs Viewer visual redesign.

Done When

  • manage index-tree alone exposes the registered selection control and stable checkbox gutter;
  • checkbox state, selected count, range anchor, and selection mode have one focused browser-state owner;
  • checkbox click and Shift-click change only the checked set, while document navigation and active-row semantics remain independent;
  • Clear, Done, scope/view exit, and reload reconciliation follow the explicit lifecycle contract;
  • same-scope reloads preserve eligible checked ids and prune missing or ineligible ids without storage persistence;
  • all existing single-document controls and current drag/drop retain their pre-delivery targets and behavior;
  • selecting documents performs no network request, mutation, rebuild, cache invalidation, or busy transition;
  • focused automated evidence covers durable state, targeting, DOM wiring, and route boundaries, with visual and keyboard feel left to user review;
  • Docs Viewer Runtime or Runtime Module Ownership records the shipped owner before this delivery closes.

Follow-On Order

  1. Prove this selection foundation.
  2. Use the checked-document contract for the separately approved Document Sharing UI Redesign and its completed Package Prepare deliveries.
  3. Create and approve a separate group-movement delivery if that consumer is chosen. Group drag begins only from a checked document; dragging an unchecked document remains the current single-node move.

This delivery did not itself authorize either follow-on; Package Prepare was approved and delivered separately, while group movement remains unapproved.
